question: The scatter plot shows rainfall vs. crop yield. Which statement is correct according to the chart data?

options.A: Higher rainfall values are associated with clearly lower crop yields.
options.B: There is a generally positive relationship between rainfall and yield up to a point, after which yields start to decline.
options.C: There is no visible relationship between rainfall and crop yield.
options.D: Yields are highest at the very lowest rainfall values shown.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the x-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
